title: 10 best herbal teas for psoriasis
intro_desc: Herbal teas for psoriasis are infusions or decoctions made from various dried herbs, flowers, and roots, which can help alleviate psoriasis symptoms. These teas can have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immune-modulating properties, contributing to the reduction of redness, itching, and scaling associated with psoriasis. Examples of herbal teas that can benefit individuals with psoriasis include chamomile, calendula, burdock root, and milk thistle. By incorporating these teas into their daily routine, psoriasis patients may experience improvements in their quality of life, such as reduced discomfort, fewer flare-ups, and enhanced mental well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with psoriasis because it contains anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can alleviate the symptoms of this skin condition. The herb's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, can help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with psoriasis. Additionally, yarrow's antiseptic properties can help prevent infections in broken skin caused by scratching or picking at psoriasis lesions. Furthermore, yarrow tea has been found to promote wound healing and skin regeneration, which can help improve the overall appearance of the skin and reduce the severity of psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, help reduce inflammation associated with psoriasis.
Antipruritic: The antipruritic effect of yarrow tea, attributed to its alkaloids and lactones, alleviates itching, a common symptom of psoriasis.
Antimicrobial: Yarrow tea's antimicrobial properties, owing to the presence of volatile oils like azulene, help prevent skin infections that can worsen psoriasis.
Wound healing: The wound healing properties of yarrow tea, attributed to its tannins and saponins, promote skin tissue regeneration and help manage psoriasis lesions.

Immune modulation: Yarrow tea's ability to modulate the immune system, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, can help regulate the overactive immune response that contributes to psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water in a pot, then add 2-3 teaspoons of dried yarrow flowers and leaves.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the yarrow herbs, then let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink 2-3 cups of yarrow tea daily for best results in managing psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing psoriasis symptoms.
Leaves: Contain sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to help with skin conditions like psoriasis.
Seeds: High in alpha-linolenic acid, an essential fatty acid that may help with skin inflammation.
Stems: Contain flavonoids and alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties beneficial for psoriasis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tea helps with psoriasis because it contains mucilage, a substance that forms a soothing film over the skin, reducing redness and irritation. This film also helps to lock in moisture, keeping the skin hydrated and supple. Additionally, marshmallow tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate the swelling and discomfort associated with psoriasis. The tea is also rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the skin from further damage caused by free radicals. Furthermore, it is believed that marshmallow tea may help regulate the immune system, which could potentially reduce the frequency and severity of psoriasis flare-ups.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Marshmallow tea contains mucilage, which helps reduce inflammation and soothes irritated skin, providing relief for psoriasis symptoms.
Demulcent: The demulcent properties of marshmallow tea, due to its mucilage content, create a protective layer on the skin, helping to alleviate psoriasis-related dryness and discomfort.

Wound healing: Marshmallow tea has been shown to promote wound healing, which can be beneficial for managing psoriasis lesions and reducing the risk of infection. This is attributed to the tea's flavonoid and phenolic acid content.
Antioxidant: Marshmallow tea contains antioxidants that help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to psoriasis development and severity.
Immune system support: The tea's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties can help modulate the immune response, potentially reducing the overactive immune response associated with psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of dried marshmallow root with 4 cups of water, and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, and let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried chamomile flowers, and let it steep for an additional 5 minutes.
Strain the tea, and discard the solids.
Drink 2-3 cups of the marshmallow tea daily, preferably between meals, for best results.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant contain mucilage, which can help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin, making it useful for treating psoriasis.
Leaves: The leaves of the marshmallow plant also contain mucilage and are used to make medicinal teas to help relieve the itching and inflammation associated with psoriasis.
Flowers: The flowers of the marshmallow plant contain flavonoids and antioxidants, which can help reduce redness and swelling, making them a valuable addition to teas for treating psoriasis.
Seeds: The seeds of the marshmallow plant are rich in mucilage, which can help moisturize and soothe dry, flaky skin, a common symptom of psoriasis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock tea helps with psoriasis because it has anti-inflammatory and blood purifying properties that can alleviate symptoms of this skin condition. Burdock is rich in essential fatty acids, vitamins, and minerals that nourish the skin and support its natural healing process. Its anti-inflammatory properties can reduce redness, swelling, and itching, while its blood purifying abilities help eliminate toxins from the body that can trigger psoriasis outbreaks. Drinking burdock tea regularly can help soothe and calm irritated skin, promoting a clearer and healthier complexion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Burdock tea's anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to its rich supply of phenolic acids and quercetin, help alleviate psoriasis-related redness, swelling, and discomfort.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content, mainly due to the presence of phenolic acids and flavonoids, in burdock tea aids in neutralizing free radicals, thereby potentially reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with psoriasis.

Blood purification: Burdock tea's blood-purifying properties, linked to its abundance in arctiin and lignans, may assist in eliminating toxins from the body, which could contribute to the improvement of skin conditions like psoriasis.

Immunomodulatory: The immunomodulatory effects of burdock tea, mediated by its polysaccharides and tannins, can help regulate the immune response, thus mitigating the overactive immune response seen in psoriasis.
Demulcent: Burdock tea's demulcent properties, derived from its mucilaginous compounds, can help soothe and protect irritated skin, providing relief for psoriasis-affected areas.
remedy_recipe: In a saucepan, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried burdock root.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the burdock root pieces.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 2-3 cups of burdock tea daily, preferably between meals, for best results in managing psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Burdock roots are commonly used in medicinal teas for psoriasis due to their anti-inflammatory and blood purifying properties.
Leaves: Burdock leaves are rich in antioxidants and have been used in teas to help soothe irritated skin and reduce redness associated with psoriasis.
Seeds: Burdock seeds are known for their detoxifying effects, making them a popular addition to teas that aim to treat skin conditions like psoriasis.
Flowers: Burdock flowers contain beneficial compounds that can help alleviate symptoms of psoriasis, such as itching and flaking, when used in medicinal teas.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with psoriasis because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can alleviate symptoms of the skin condition. The herbal tea is rich in flavonoids and carotenoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve skin health. Additionally, pot marigold tea has been found to stimulate the immune system, helping to combat the overactive immune response that can contribute to psoriasis symptoms. Drinking pot marigold tea may also help to soothe irritated skin and promote the healing of lesions, providing relief for those suffering from psoriasis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea has potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and redness in psoriasis lesions, primarily due to the presence of flavonoids and carotenoids.
Wound-healing: The tea promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, beneficial for treating psoriasis-related skin damage, thanks to the active constituents like triterpenes and polysaccharides.

Antipruritic: Pot marigold tea alleviates itching, a common and distressing symptom of psoriasis, owing to its antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties, found in its flavonoid and alkaloid content.

Antimicrobial: The tea inhibits the growth of bacteria and fungi, helping maintain skin health and preventing secondary infections in psoriasis-affected areas, attributed to its essential oils and tannins.

Immunomodulatory: Pot marigold tea regulates the immune system, moderating the overactive immune response that contributes to psoriasis development, due to the presence of active compounds like flavonoids, saponins, and polysaccharides.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried pot marigold flowers.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the flowers, leaving only the liquid.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 2-3 cups of this pot marigold tea daily for best results in managing psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, pot marigold flowers are used to soothe irritated skin and reduce redness associated with psoriasis.
Leaves: Containing high levels of flavonoids and carotenoids, pot marigold leaves are used in medicinal teas to alleviate symptoms of psoriasis, such as itching and scaling.
Seeds: Packed with essential fatty acids, pot marigold seeds are used to make teas that help reduce inflammation and promote skin healing in individuals with psoriasis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea helps with psoriasis because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with the skin condition. The tea's antioxidants, including flavonoids and polyphenols, can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and environmental stressors, while its anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe and calm irritated skin. Additionally, white tea has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms. Overall, white tea's unique combination of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory agents, and antimicrobial properties make it a promising natural remedy for those looking to manage and alleviate psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with psoriasis, thanks to the presence of flavonoids and polyphenols.
Antioxidant: White tea contains high levels of antioxidants that can help protect skin cells from damage, reducing the severity of psoriasis symptoms, due to the high concentration of catechins.

Antimicrobial: White tea has antimicrobial properties that may help control the growth of bacteria and fungi on the skin, potentially reducing the risk of infections in psoriasis-prone areas, attributed to the tannins and caffeine.

Immune system support: White tea can help boost the immune system, which may help regulate the overactive immune response that contributes to psoriasis, owing to the presence of flavonoids, polyphenols, and epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG).

Skin cell regeneration: White tea promotes skin cell regeneration and can help maintain the skin's natural barrier function, allowing it to heal and protect itself from irritation, partly due to the action of EGCG and other antioxidants.
remedy_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water and let it cool to 175-185°F, then pour over 1 teaspoon of white tea leaves and steep for 2-3 minutes.
While the tea is steeping, finely chop 1 tablespoon of chamomile flowers and 1 tablespoon of calendula petals.
After the tea has steeped, strain out the tea leaves and add the chopped herbs to the hot tea.
Let the herbs steep for an additional 5-minutes, then strain out the herbs and discard.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, for relief of psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, white tea leaves help reduce inflammation and soothe psoriasis symptoms.
Buds: High in anti-inflammatory properties, white tea buds can help alleviate redness and irritation caused by psoriasis.
Flowers: Containing essential oils, white tea flowers can help moisturize and relieve dry, flaky skin associated with psoriasis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cannabis tea helps with psoriasis because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with the skin condition. The tea is made from the leaves of the cannabis plant, which are rich in cannabinoids, terpenes, and flavonoids that work together to alleviate symptoms of psoriasis. The cannabinoids in cannabis tea, such as CBD and THC, interact with the body's endocannabinoid system to regulate immune response and reduce inflammation. Additionally, the tea's antioxidant properties can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall skin health. By drinking cannabis tea, individuals with psoriasis may experience relief from itching, pain, and inflammation, leading to improved skin appearance and quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Cannabis tea contains cannabinoids like THC and CBD that help reduce inflammation, which is a key factor in psoriasis symptoms.
Immunomodulatory: Certain cannabinoids, such as CBD, can help regulate the immune system, potentially reducing overactive immune responses that lead to psoriasis flare-ups.
Analgesic: The pain-relieving properties of cannabis tea, derived from cannabinoids like THC and CBD, can help alleviate discomfort associated with psoriasis.
Antipruritic: Cannabis tea may help reduce itching, a common and distressing symptom of psoriasis, due to the anti-inflammatory and soothing effects of cannabinoids.
Antiproliferative: Cannabinoids like CBD and THC have been shown to inhibit keratinocyte proliferation, which may help slow the rapid skin cell growth and buildup characteristic of psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 gram of dried cannabis flower, rich in CBD, and 2 teaspoons of chamomile flowers.
Grind the cannabis and chamomile together in a grinder until finely ground.
Place the ground mixture into a tea infuser and steep in a boiling pot of water for 15-20 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser and stir in 1 teaspoon of honey and a squeeze of lemon juice for taste.
Consume the tea while warm, 1-2 times daily, to potentially help alleviate psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, cannabis leaves can help alleviate psoriasis symptoms.
Flowers: Containing high levels of cannabinoids and terpenes, cannabis flowers can reduce inflammation and pain associated with psoriasis.
Seeds: Packed with essential fatty acids and nutrients, hemp seeds can help improve skin health and reduce psoriasis symptoms.
Buds: High in cannabinoids and terpenes, cannabis buds can help regulate the immune system and reduce inflammation in psoriasis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with psoriasis because it contains anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate the redness, swelling, and pain associated with psoriasis, while its antioxidants can help protect the skin from further damage. Additionally, German chamomile tea has been shown to have a calming effect on the skin, which can help reduce the itching and discomfort associated with psoriasis. Drinking the tea or using it as a compress can provide relief and promote healing of the affected areas. However, it is important to note that while German chamomile tea can help alleviate symptoms of psoriasis, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tea contains the active constituent chamazulene, which helps reduce inflammation in psoriasis by inhibiting the production of inflammatory chemicals in the body.
Antipruritic: The flavonoids in German chamomile tea, such as apigenin and luteolin, have antipruritic properties that help alleviate itching, a common symptom of psoriasis.

Antioxidant: German chamomile tea is rich in antioxidants, including terpenoids and flavonoids, which help protect skin cells from oxidative stress and damage, contributing to the overall health and integrity of the skin in psoriasis.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of German chamomile tea, due to its essential oils and other active constituents, can help maintain a healthy skin microbiome and prevent infections, which can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms.

Wound healing: German chamomile tea promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, thanks to its flavonoids, essential oils, and other active constituents, which can help soothe and repair damaged skin in psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2-3 teaspoons of dried German chamomile flowers.
Place the chamomile flowers in a tea infuser or teapot.
Boil water and pour it over the chamomile flowers, allowing them to steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea to remove the flowers.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day for best results.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, chamomile flowers are used in medicinal teas for psoriasis due to their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and soothing properties.
Leaves: Containing volatile oils and antioxidants, chamomile leaves are used in medicinal teas for psoriasis to help reduce inflammation and provide relief from itching and irritation.
Seeds: Chamomile seeds are a source of essential oils and antioxidants, making them useful in medicinal teas for psoriasis to help alleviate redness, swelling, and discomfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chicory tea helps with psoriasis because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can reduce skin irritation and inflammation. Chicory root contains polyphenols and flavonoids, which are known to help soothe skin conditions like psoriasis. The tea can help stimulate the production of bile in the liver, which aids in digestion and can help reduce toxins in the body. Since psoriasis is believed to be linked to problems with the immune system and can be triggered by environmental factors, reducing toxins and inflammation in the body may help alleviate psoriasis symptoms. Additionally, chicory tea is caffeine-free and can be a soothing, comforting beverage for those experiencing skin discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chicory tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like sesquiterpene lactones, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate psoriasis symptoms.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content, including polyphenols and flavonoids, in chicory tea can help protect skin cells from damage and support overall skin health in psoriasis.
Immune modulation: Chicory tea's ability to modulate the immune system, partly due to its polysaccharides, can help regulate the overactive immune response seen in psoriasis.
Wound healing: Chicory tea's active constituents, such as tannins and phenolic acids, promote wound healing and tissue regeneration, which can be beneficial for managing psoriasis lesions.

Detoxification: Chicory tea supports detoxification processes in the body, partly due to its sesquiterpene lactones and phenolic acids, which can help eliminate toxins and reduce the burden on the body, potentially alleviating psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Boil one cup of water in a pot for 3-5 minutes.
Add one teaspoon of dried chicory herb to a tea infuser or strainer.
Pour the boiling water over the chicory herb and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strainer and discard the used herb.
Drink the chicory tea while it's still warm, up to three cups a day.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chicory roots are commonly used in medicinal teas for psoriasis due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Leaves: Chicory leaves are rich in vitamins and minerals, making them a popular choice for soothing psoriasis symptoms.
Flowers: Chicory flowers contain flavonoids and other beneficial compounds that can help reduce redness and irritation associated with psoriasis.
Seeds: Chicory seeds are a great source of essential fatty acids, which can help alleviate dryness and scaling in individuals with psoriasis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Old man's beard tea helps with psoriasis because it contains high levels of antioxidants, flavonoids, and essential oils that have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. These compounds can help reduce redness, irritation, and swelling associated with psoriasis. Additionally, old man's beard tea has been shown to have immune-modulating effects, which can help regulate the overactive immune response that contributes to psoriasis symptoms. Drinking this tea regularly may also help improve skin hydration and promote overall skin health, leading to a reduction in psoriasis flare-ups.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Old man's beard tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with psoriasis, due to the presence of phenolic acids and flavonoids.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in old man's beard tea helps protect skin cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms, thanks to its rich supply of polyphenols and vitamin C.

Immunomodulatory: Old man's beard tea can help regulate the immune system, reducing the overactive immune response that leads to psoriasis symptoms, due to the presence of polysaccharides and lectins.
Antipruritic: Old man's beard tea has antipruritic properties that help alleviate itching, a common and distressing symptom of psoriasis, because of the presence of tannins and saponins.

Wound healing: The wound healing properties of old man's beard tea promote skin regeneration and help repair the damaged skin caused by psoriasis, thanks to the presence of triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids.
remedy_recipe: In a stovepot, combine 1 cup of dried old man's beard herb (Usnea lichen) with 4 cups of water.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herb, then let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Stir in 1-2 teaspoons of honey for added anti-inflammatory benefits and improved taste.
Drink 2-3 cups of the tea daily, preferably between meals, for at least a month to see improvements in psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in triterpenoid saponins, the leaves of old man's beard plant are used to make medicinal teas for psoriasis due to their anti-inflammatory and skin-soothing properties.
Flowers: Containing high levels of flavonoids, old man's beard flowers are used in teas to help reduce redness and irritation associated with psoriasis.
Stems: High in rosmarinic acid, the stems of old man's beard plant are used to make medicinal teas for psoriasis to help alleviate itching and improve skin health.
Seeds: Packed with essential fatty acids, old man's beard seeds are used in teas for psoriasis to help reduce skin inflammation and promote skin repair.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tea helps with psoriasis because it is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as silicon, selenium, and various flavonoids. These components can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the skin, which are common symptoms of psoriasis. Additionally, horsetail tea may help promote the shedding of dead skin cells and the growth of new, healthy skin cells, which can be beneficial for individuals with psoriasis. The antioxidants in horsetail tea can also help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can further exacerbate psoriasis symptoms. Overall, horsetail tea can provide a natural and holistic approach to managing psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help reduce inflammation associated with psoriasis. This is important because psoriasis is characterized by red, inflamed skin and reducing inflammation can help alleviate symptoms.

Antioxidant: Horsetail tea is rich in antioxidants, including silica and selenium, which can help protect skin cells from damage caused by free radicals. This is important for psoriasis because oxidative stress can exacerbate inflammation and contribute to the development of psoriasis.

Antimicrobial: Horsetail tea has antimicrobial properties, due to the presence of compounds like tannins and essential oils, which can help prevent infections and promote wound healing. This is important for psoriasis because open sores and lesions can be prone to infection, leading to further complications.

Astringent: Horsetail tea has astringent properties, which can help tighten and dry out inflamed skin. This is important for psoriasis because it can help reduce the appearance of scaly, silvery patches and soothe itchiness.

Circulation-boosting: Horsetail tea contains compounds that can help improve circulation, such as flavonoids and saponins. This is important for psoriasis because improved blood flow can help promote skin healing and reduce the buildup of dead skin cells that contribute to psoriasis plaques.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and add 1-2 teaspoons of dried horsetail herb, let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herb particles.
Squeeze half a lemon into the tea for added vitamin C.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of raw honey for sweetness and additional skin-healing benefits.
Consume the tea while warm, 2-3 times a day for best results.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: The underground stem of horsetail plant is rich in silica and other minerals, making it a popular choice for strengthening connective tissue and reducing inflammation associated with psoriasis.
Stems: Horsetail stems contain high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help soothe irritated skin and reduce redness in psoriasis.
Leaves: The leaves of horsetail plant have diuretic properties and are believed to help eliminate toxins from the body, which can be beneficial for managing psoriasis symptoms.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat psoriasis with herbal teas is by regularly consuming tea made from chamomile, calendula, or burdock root. These teas have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritated skin and reduce redness. Additionally, drinking tea made from milk thistle or dandelion can help support liver function, which is important for overall skin health. It's important to note that while herbal teas can be a helpful complement to a treatment plan, they should not be used as a substitute for medical advice or treatment from a healthcare professional.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to psoriasis that are treated with herbal teas include eczema and dermatitis. Herbal teas like chamomile, calendula, and nettle have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritated skin and reduce redness and itching. Burdock root tea is known to purify the blood and help eliminate toxins, which can be beneficial for skin conditions like psoriasis and eczema. Milk thistle tea is also used to treat skin conditions as it supports liver function and helps detoxify the body. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal remedies.
